返回本批次

世界上最小的电子阅读器，你可以自己制作一个

The tiniest e-reader in the world, and you can build one yourself

www.androidauthority.com 作者 Brajeshwar 分数 60 评论 17 2026/04/16 00:53
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. YouTube / 保罗·拉吉尔TL;DR
  2. 一位 YouTuber 制作了一款带有 3D 打印外壳和 DIY 电子产品的微型电子阅读器。这款电子阅读器仅比成人拇指稍大，可容纳大约 6 到 10 本书。创建者估计建造它的成本约为 30 美元。
  3. 尽管电子阅读器已经问世近二十年，但它在很大程度上仅限于一小部分用户，他们需要专为阅读而设计的无干扰体验。虽然电子阅读器远没有智能手机那么受欢迎，但最近人们对电子阅读器的兴趣激增，尤其是那些希望摆脱海量通知的人。但尽管需求量很大，但它们还不能完全取代智能手机，并且被降级为辅助设备的类别，想要随身携带它们可能会引发大多数人无法轻易鼓起的决心。但 YouTuber 可能已经通过制造一款袖珍型电子阅读器解决了这个问题，这种电子阅读器你无法轻松随身携带到任何地方，甚至感觉不到它的存在。YouTuber Paul Lagier 最近记录了他们从头开始构建紧凑型电子阅读器的过程，该电子阅读器几乎与汽车钥匙扣大小相同。这款微型设备可以让您在活动之间打发时间，而不是将阅读视为一项专门的活动，从而减少了其他设备的吸引力。这款微型电子阅读器并没有走一些带有电子墨水显示屏的 Android 设备的路线，提供广泛的应用程序支持和成为智能手机的潜力，而是专注于让你只是阅读。更令人兴奋的是 Lagier 自己构建了该设备。这包括设计和 3D 打印外壳、使用 ESP32 微控制器组装逻辑板等关键组件，并将其连接到电池和 Heltec 无线纸显示器。在后续视频中，创作者分享了各个方面的迭代更新、改进固件，甚至为任何想要构建自己的小型电子阅读器的人提供了资源。新版本带来了设计改进，可以轻松在各种 3D 打印机上进行打印。侧面的触觉按钮也略有调整，但最大的改进来自于固件更改的形式。除了小屏幕上更好的可视性之外，创建者还增强了用于同步电子书和存储书签的网络界面。另一个重大改进是添加了文件夹，这将使用户可以将各种书籍分为不同的类别。是否根据类型、长度、难度或任何其他方面对它们进行排序取决于您。更新的固件还允许您用自定义屏幕保护程序替换屏幕保护程序。固件也进行了更新，以获得更好的电源效率、更多语言和更好的系统稳定性。由于 ESP32 仅支持 8MB 板载存储，因此创建者还优化了系统文件使用的空间，为书籍腾出更多空间。据他们介绍，它现在可以容纳 6 到 10 本书，大约 300 页。请注意，这些书籍是简单的 TXT 格式，而不是 PDF 或 EPUB。最后，还添加了新的列表功能，因此您不仅可以保存要阅读的标题，还可以保存待办事项列表。创建者在其 Ko-Fi 上共享了所有文件和说明，您可以花费 4.90 欧元（约 5.80 美元）解锁它们。您还可以访问所有未来的更新，而无需再次付费。再加上灯丝、显示屏和电池模块的大约 30 美元估计，您就可以以基本 Kindle 的五分之一的价格获得一个非常有用的阅读配件。关注感谢您成为我们社区的一员。发帖前请阅读我们的评论政策。

Original Summary

  1. YouTube / Paul LagierTL;DR
  2. A YouTuber has built a tiny e-reader with a 3D-printed shell and DIY electronics. The e-reader, only slightly bigger than an adult human thumb, can hold roughly six to 10 books. The creator estimates that the cost of building it is around $30.
  3. Despite being available for nearly two decades, e-readers have largely been limited to a small sect of users who demand a distraction-free experience tailored for reading. While they have been nowhere near as popular as smartphones, e-readers have recently seen a surge in interest, especially among people who wish to escape the deluge of notifications. But despite being in high demand, they can’t fully replace smartphones (yet), and are reduced to the category of secondary devices, and wanting to carry them around might elicit more determination than most people can easily muster. But a YouTuber may have solved this problem by building a pocket-friendly e-reader that you can’t easily carry with you everywhere without even feeling its presence.YouTuber Paul Lagier recently documented their process of building a compact e-reader, nearly the size of a car keyfob, from scratch. The tiny device diminished the pull of other devices by letting you fill the time between activities, rather than treating reading as a dedicated action. And instead of taking the route of some Android-based devices with E-Ink displays that offer wide app support and the potential to be a smartphone, the tiny e-reader specializes in letting you just read.What’s more exciting is that Lagier built the device by themselves. That involves designing and 3D printing the outer shell, assembling key components like the logic board, with an ESP32 microcontroller, and attaching it to a battery and a Heltec Wireless Paper display. In a follow-up video, the creator has shared updates on iterating on various aspects, improving the firmware, and even resources for anyone who wants to build their own tiny e-reader.The newer version brings design improvements that make it easy to print on a diverse set of 3D printers. The tactile button on the side is slightly adjusted, too, but the biggest improvement comes in the form of the firmware changes. Along with better visibility on the tiny screen, the creator has enhanced the web interface that is used to sync ebooks and store bookmarks. Another big improvement is the addition of folders, which will let users segment various books into different categories. It’s up to you whether you sort them based on genre, length, difficulty, or any other aspect.The updated firmware also lets you replace the screensaver with a custom one. The firmware has also been updated for better power efficiency, more languages, and better system stability.Since the ESP32 only supports 8MB of onboard storage, the creator has also optimized the space used by system files to make more room for books. According to them, it can now fit six to 10 books with roughly 300 pages. Note that these books are in simple TXT format and not PDF or EPUB. Finally, a new lists feature was also added, so you can not only save the titles you want to read but also your to-do lists.The creator has shared all the files and instructions on their Ko-Fi, and you can unlock them for €4.90 (~$5.80). You’ll also have access to all the future updates without having to pay again. Add that to the roughly $30 estimate for filament, display, and the battery module, and you have a pretty useful reading accessory for about a fifth of a basic Kindle.FollowThank you for being part of our community. Read our Comment Policy before posting.

评论（默认中文）

randusername 2026/04/16 01:16
小型电子阅读器的真正优点是您无需水平移动眼睛即可阅读。

如果每行只有四个单词，您可以将眼睛向中间滑动，像正常快速阅读一样将所有单词集中起来。

我有 xteink X4 和 X3，我发誓我可以毫无压力地阅读更长时间。您还可以在大多数其他电子阅读器上大幅提高利润。

What's really nice about tiny e-readers is that you can read without having to move your eyes horizontally.

If there are only four words per line, you can slide your eyes down the middle and take in all the words in a chunk like you would for normal speed-reading.

I have an xteink X4 and X3 and I swear I can read for much longer without strain. You can also just crank your margins way up on most other e-readers.

在 HN 中查看
Minor49er 2026/04/16 02:10
是否真的值得权衡拥有更小的存储空间、缩小的屏幕尺寸以及更频繁地点击，只是为了不必水平移动眼睛太多？
Is it really worth the tradeoffs of having significantly smaller storage space, reduced screen size, and clicking through way more often, just to not have to move your eyes as much horizontally?
在 HN 中查看
piffey 2026/04/16 04:00
对我来说似乎是。我把它放在衬衫前面的口袋里，随时拿出来阅读。今年到目前为止，我已经读了 8 本书。考虑到随身携带 Kobo 非常方便，这比我去年这个时候在 Kobo 上读到的书多了 7 本书。诚然，我通常是纸质书阅读器，所以我的 Kobo 一直只是一个旅行设备，但 Xteink 可以让您轻松地在任何地方阅读。
For me seems to be. I keep it in my front shirt pocket, pull it out to read all the time. I've gone through 8 books on it so far this year. That's 7 more books than I read on my Kobo by this time last year given how convenient it is to take it everywhere. Granted I'm usually a paper book reader so my Kobo was always just a travel device, but the Xteink makes it so easy to squeeze reading in everywhere.
在 HN 中查看
postalponed 2026/04/16 03:14
对我来说最大的好处是设备的尺寸。我总是带着它，所以我更喜欢阅读而不是末日卷轴
The biggest benefit for me is the size of the device. I always have it on me, so I rather read than doomscroll
在 HN 中查看
alistairSH 2026/04/16 03:19
等到年纪大了，需要加大字体大小的时候就可以了。 现在，每行只有 2 个单词，并且通过不断点击页面，您会得到腕管或 De Quervain's。 我只是半开玩笑。
Just wait until you get older and need to increase the font size. Now you're down to 2 words/line and you get carpal tunnel or De Quervain's from the constant clicking to page. I'm only half kidding.
在 HN 中查看
steve-atx-7600 2026/04/16 08:32
我同意，但我会使用 epub 和 iPhone 应用程序调整文本以提高可读性。 考虑到现在 iPhone 的屏幕有多好，我看不出有什么理由需要另一款设备。
I agree but I adjust text like this for readability using epubs and iPhone apps. I don’t see a reason for another device given how good iPhone screens are now.
在 HN 中查看
broabprobe 2026/04/16 01:23
同意，x3 令人难以置信。您将支付 2 倍的费用，但 Crosspoint 的用户体验非常出色
Agreed, the x3 is incredible. You'll pay 2x what this costs but the ux is phenomenal with Crosspoint
在 HN 中查看
fmajid 2026/04/16 03:53
请尝试使用 M5Stack Paper S3。我正在我的上运行 Crosspoint Reader 的社区版本，它实际上有一个触摸屏和一个 microSD 读卡器，据说限制为 16GB，但可以很好地处理我的 128GB 卡。
Try the M5Stack Paper S3 instead. I am running a community build of Crosspoint Reader on mine, it actually has a touchscreen and a microSD card reader, supposedly limited to 16GB but handles my 128GB card just fine.
在 HN 中查看
ghostly_s 2026/04/16 03:50
我无法想象这个小东西能够刷新得足够快以跟上我的阅读速度（不是疯狂的快）。
I can't imagine this tiny thing being able to refresh fast enough to keep up with my reading speed (not crazy fast).
在 HN 中查看
Uncle_Brumpus 2026/04/16 02:18
我现在的电子阅读器是由 Oddly Specific Objects 的 Joey Castillo 设计的 Openbook Abridged。它比这个微型设备大，但比智能手机之类的东西小，我认为它的尺寸非常完美。这是一种类似的设备，实际上功能比 O24 还要少。我非常喜欢它，自从大约 6 个月前将套件焊接在一起以来，我读的书比以前多了很多。
My current e-reader is an Openbook Abridged designed by Joey Castillo of Oddly Specific Objects. It is larger than this tiny device, but smaller than something like a smartphone, and I think it is the PERFECT size. It's a similar kind of device which actually has even less features than this O24. I love it so much, and have been reading so much more than I used to since I soldered the kit together ~6 months ago.
在 HN 中查看
baldgeek 2026/04/16 02:30
这真的出了吗？去年我在 Supercon 上看到了他的演示装置。我很想买一个
Is this actually out? I saw him with demo units at Supercon last year. I would love to get one
在 HN 中查看
ofrzeta 2026/04/16 03:37
“由于 ESP32 仅支持 8MB 板载存储”——带有 SPI 接口的 SD 卡分线成本为 2 美元。这将是一个简单的补充。我认为还有带 SD 卡插槽的 ESP32 板，但这也可以制作出漂亮的定制 PCB。
"Since the ESP32 only supports 8MB of onboard storage" - an SD card breakout with SPI interface costs 2 bucks. That would be an easy addition. I think there are also ESP32 boards with SD card slot but this would also make for a nice custom PCB.
在 HN 中查看
Lord_Zero 2026/04/16 03:55
等等，他们是否建议使用 Heltec Lora ESP32 板？这是一个超级奇怪的选择。 Adafruit 有您所说的主板，它们也有三色，并且具有相同外形规格的更多功能。为什么要购买 Heltec Lora 板用作电子阅读器并浪费金钱在其上安装完美的 Lora 芯片......
Wait, are they suggesting using the Heltec Lora ESP32 board? That is a super odd choice. Adafruit has the boards you speak of and they have tri-color too and more features in the same form factor. Why buy a Heltec Lora board to use as an e-reader and waste money on a perfectly good Lora chip on it...
在 HN 中查看
ofrzeta 2026/04/16 04:44
我想我们正在使用 Heltec 无线纸板 https://heltec.org/project/wireless-paper/
I think were using the Heltec Wireless Paper board https://heltec.org/project/wireless-paper/
在 HN 中查看
fmajid 2026/04/16 03:51
我不太喜欢 eInk，尝试过 PocketBook Verse 电子阅读器（比 Kindle 更开放，有一个 microSD 插槽，可容纳我的 70+GB 完全无 DRM 的电子书收藏），屏幕颗粒感相当大。

最近关于 HN 的一篇文章说服我尝试 M5Stack Paper S3。更好的屏幕，16 级灰度触摸屏，microSD 插槽，ESP32-S3 带 WiFi 和蓝牙，支持 Crosspoint Reader 软件。它有一个磁性背面，但不能像 Xteink4 那样安装在 Magsafe 手机背面。虽然价格贵了一倍，但还是很值得。

I'm not that fond of eInk, having tried a PocketBook Verse e-reader (more open than a Kindle, has a microSD slot for my 70+GB fully DRM-free eBook collection) with a fairly grainy screen.

A recent article on HN convinced me to try the M5Stack Paper S3. Much nicer screen with 16-level grayscale touchscreen, microSD slot, ESP32-S3 with WiFi and Bluetooth, supports the Crosspoint Reader software. It's got a magnetic back but won't fit on the back of a Magsafe phone like the Xteink4 can. It's double the price, but well worth it.

在 HN 中查看
forsalebypwner 2026/04/16 02:07
我觉得这篇文章是专门为我写的，因为我有一些用于 Pi Zero 的 Waveshare ePaper HAT 目前未使用，除了我应该与 ESP32 一起使用的 HAT 接口之外，它们还有标准 GPIO 连接，因为电池寿命比为此运行完整的 Linux 安装重要得多！
I feel like this article was made for me specifically because I have a handful of Waveshare ePaper HATs for the Pi Zero that are currently sitting unused, they have standard GPIO connections in addition to the HAT interface that I should use along with an ESP32, because battery life would be much more important than running a full linux install for this!
